steps.title: Fit the shown line from two points
steps.content: The graph shown is $y=f(x)-11$. Use a Desmos table with two clear points from the graph: $(-1,2)$ and $(0,-2)$. A two-point line calculation gives slope $-4$ and intercept $-2$, so the shown line is $y=-4x-2$.

steps.title: Write the equation of the graphed line
steps.content: The table/calculated result gives the equation directly: $$y=-4x-2$$ This is the graph of $y=f(x)-11$, not the graph of $y=f(x)$.

steps.title: Solve for f(x) using the given relationship
steps.content: The problem states the graph shows $y = f(x) - 11$. Substituting our equation: $$-4x - 2 = f(x) - 11$$ Adding $11$ to both sides: $$f(x) = -4x - 2 + 11 = -4x + 9$$ That matches choice B — B is correct.
